events.title: Triangle Pop-Up at Raleigh Iron Works
events.summary: A monthly pop-up market on the second Saturday of each month featuring a rotating roster of local makers, artisans, and live music from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. A low-key, all-ages way to discover local makers and the Raleigh creative community in a converted industrial space.

events.title: Live After 5 Outdoor Concert Series
events.location: Moore Square / Raleigh Union Station / Seaboard Station, Raleigh
events.summary: Free weekly outdoor concerts held six Thursdays from May 14 through August 13, 2026, starting at 7 p.m. at rotating downtown Raleigh venues. A great no-cost way to experience Raleigh's walkable downtown neighborhoods and hear live music with neighbors.

events.title: Got to Be NC Festival
events.summary: A free, three-day (May 15–17) celebration of North Carolina food, agriculture, music, and culture held at the State Fairgrounds, with activities for all ages across multiple stages and pavilions. It's one of the best single events for newcomers to taste what makes the state tick.

events.title: Bimbé Cultural Arts Festival
events.summary: Now in its 56th year, this free Durham Parks & Recreation festival (1–7 p.m.) celebrates African and African American heritage through live music, dance, vendors, and community programming. One of the longest-running cultural festivals in the Southeast and a quintessential Durham experience.

events.title: AMA Triangle Monthly Mixer — May
events.summary: The American Marketing Association's Triangle chapter hosts a casual after-work mixer (5:30–7:30 p.m.) at Fortnight Brewing in Cary, open to marketers, entrepreneurs, and business professionals at all career levels. A relaxed way to start building a professional network in the Triangle.

events.title: Animazement
events.summary: A three-day (May 22–24) Japanese animation and pop-culture convention drawing roughly 15,000 attendees over Memorial Day weekend to the Raleigh Convention Center. Whether you're a fan of anime or just curious, the cosplay spectacle and dealers' hall alone are worth the trip.

events.title: The Market at NCMA
events.summary: A curated outdoor pop-up market on the grounds of the North Carolina Museum of Art, running 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. with local artisan vendors — free to browse, with the museum's permanent collection and park trails right next door for a full family outing.

events.title: AMA Triangle Monthly Mixer — June
events.summary: The Triangle chapter of the American Marketing Association gathers again (5:30–7:30 p.m.) at Trophy Brewing in Raleigh for a second round of professional networking in a casual taproom setting. A solid option for transplants who missed the May event or want to deepen new connections.
